name: github-oauth-nango-integration
description: Use when implementing GitHub OAuth + GitHub App authentication with Nango - provides two-connection pattern for user login and repo access with webhook handling
capabilities: Implement dual-connection OAuth pattern with Nango for GitHub login and repo access
Handle GitHub App installation webhooks for permission management
Reconcile OAuth user identity with GitHub App installations
Set up webhook signature verification for secure authentication
Create login and repo connection flows with proper session management
author: AgentWorkforce
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing TypeScript code examples for GitHub OAuth implementation. Static scanner flagged 87 false positives including template literal backticks as shell execution, SHA-256 as weak crypto, and metadata fields as C2 keywords. All findings dismissed. Skill teaches secure OAuth patterns with proper webhook signature verification.
valueStatement: Building apps that need both user login and repository access requires two different GitHub authentication mechanisms. This skill provides the two-connection OAuth pattern using Nango for secure login while maintaining granular repo permissions through GitHub App installations.
limitations: Does not include Nango server setup or deployment configuration
Requires existing Nango account and GitHub App credentials
Does not cover database implementation details beyond schema examples
Frontend code examples are for reference only

useCases.title: Build authenticated GitHub apps
useCases.description: Create applications where users can sign in with GitHub and grant repository access through a GitHub App installation

useCases.title: Implement secure OAuth flows
useCases.description: Set up production-ready authentication with proper webhook handling, signature verification, and session management

useCases.title: Connect user identity to permissions
useCases.description: Bridge the gap between OAuth user identity and GitHub App repository permissions for granular access control

promptTemplates.title: Basic setup
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I set up GitHub OAuth login with Nango using the two-connection pattern for user login and repo access?

promptTemplates.title: Webhook handling
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I handle Nango auth and sync webhooks for GitHub including signature verification and payload parsing?

promptTemplates.title: App OAuth flow
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement the GitHub App OAuth flow where users authenticate then authorize repo access through app installation

promptTemplates.title: Production security
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the security best practices for implementing GitHub OAuth with Nango including webhook signature verification and environment variables?

bestPractices: Always verify webhook signatures using HMAC-SHA256 with your NANGO_SECRET_KEY
Store login connection (nangoConnectionId) separately from temporary polling connections
Handle org approval pending states gracefully when GitHub App installation requires admin approval
antiPatterns: Using the same connection for both login and repo access
Skipping webhook signature verification in production
Not handling the 403 case for pending GitHub App organization approval

faq.question: What is the difference between GitHub OAuth and GitHub App authentication?
faq.answer: OAuth provides user identity for login while GitHub App provides granular repo permissions through installations

faq.question: What are the rate limits for Nango GitHub integration?
faq.answer: Nango handles rate limiting automatically but design your app to handle sync webhook delays gracefully

faq.question: How do I integrate this with my existing authentication system?
faq.answer: Store the nangoConnectionId in your users table and link it to your JWT or session system after webhook verification

faq.question: Is my GitHub OAuth client secret stored securely?
faq.answer: Credentials are stored in Nango Dashboard; only use webhook secret keys in your environment variables

faq.question: Why is my webhook verification failing?
faq.answer: Ensure you use the raw body for HMAC verification, not parsed JSON, and check your NANGO_SECRET_KEY matches

faq.question: How does this compare to using GitHub directly without Nango?
faq.answer: Nango provides unified OAuth handling, automatic token refresh, pre-built sync schedules, and webhook infrastructure
